requirement.original: 现在如果遇到没有实现的ticket，因为过于简单我使用/do来开发的，出现了无法close的现象。先调查为什么
requirement.clarified: Fix the two-part failure that prevents /close from working after /do direct development: (1) close.md forced-path timestamp resolution incorrectly requires qa-report to exist (which /do never creates), and (2) do.md has no documentation of the correct closing procedure.
requirement.what: Fix commands/close.md forced-path timestamp resolution to not require qa-report; add closing procedure documentation to commands/do.md
requirement.why: Users who develop simple tickets directly with /do cannot close them because /close's artifact preflight and task-id resolution both fail when the /dev artifact chain is absent

included: commands/close.md — forced-path timestamp resolution exception for qa-report
excluded: Generating synthetic dev/qa/completion artifacts from /do sessions
Any pipeline code, API endpoints, or user-triggered code paths

success_criteria: /close docs/dev/ticket-<ts>.md --force succeeds when qa-report-<ts>.json does not exist
/close <ts> --force succeeds when only ticket-<ts>.md exists (after forced-path timestamp fix)
commands/do.md contains documented closing procedure referencing path-form and /commit
constraints: Path-form forced resolution must not change (already safe)

in_user_path: commands/close.md (task-id resolution section — forced-path timestamp branch)
out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: commands/commit.md — commit's close-gate check only requires close-report ending with CLOSE: YES; it does not require qa-report. No change needed.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: User invokes /close after /do-based development; /close fails with either 'No spec identified' (bare invocation) or fails artifact preflight (timestamp invocation) because dev/qa/completion artifacts are absent
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Two compounding gaps: (a) commands/close.md task-id resolution for timestamp form requires qa-report-<ts>.json to exist ('Verify both exist') even when --force is present — but /do creates no qa-report, so the forced path inherits this verification and fails; (b) commands/do.md is hook-only and contains no guidance on the post-development closing procedure
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — design gap, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The --force escape hatch was documented to override 'QA debate and artifact checks' but the task-id resolution code (which runs before the forced short-circuit in close.md line 64-65) was not carved out as a separate branch. The 'same resolution rules' language caused the forced path to inherit qa-report verification.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The forced path's explicit intent is to allow closing without artifacts. Requiring qa-report in the task-id resolution step contradicts that intent. The user cannot satisfy the qa-report requirement without running /dev, which defeats the purpose of using /do.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Present since the forced-path escape hatch was added (pre-existing design gap)
observations: commands/close.md:147 verified by Read — 'Also resolve docs/dev/qa-report-${ARGUMENTS}.json. Verify both exist.' applies for timestamp form
commands/close.md:64-65 verified by Read — forced path reuses same resolution: 'Resolve the task-id from the remaining argument using the same Task-id resolution rules below'
commands/close.md:159-165 verified by Read — normal-path preflight lists all 5 required artifacts including qa-report with nested qa.status==pass
commands/close.md:188 verified by Read — close.md already has /do awareness in inspector diff section: 'orchestrator-direct edits under /do'
commands/do.md:1-7 verified by Read — body is hook-only, no post-development workflow documented
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred — runtime behavior not observed via Playwright; task involves doc and logic files not UI)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Read commands/close.md and commands/do.md in full. The task-id resolution section (lines 143-150) and forced-path section (lines 62-65) pinpoint the logic gap. The /do body (lines 1-7) confirms no workflow documentation exists.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — design gap, not a regression introduced by a specific commit

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: 出现了无法close的现象 (there is a phenomenon where they can't be closed)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: GIVEN ticket-<ts>.md exists AND qa-report-<ts>.json does NOT exist, WHEN user runs /close <ts> --force, THEN close succeeds and writes close-report-<ts>.md ending with CLOSE: YES (FORCED)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: 先调查为什么 (investigate why first)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: GIVEN commands/do.md, WHEN dev reads the file, THEN a section documents the /close --force closing procedure with explicit path form and /commit next step

component_chain.role: Hook-only slash command that bypasses orchestrator-gate. Writes /tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-<session-id>.flag. No artifact production.
Forced-path task-id resolution — reuses same resolution rules as normal path
component_chain.evidence: Read commands/close.md line 64-65: 'Resolve the task-id from the remaining argument using the same Task-id resolution rules below'

component_chain.role: Task-id resolution rules — timestamp form requires qa-report to exist
component_chain.evidence: Read commands/close.md line 147: 'Also resolve docs/dev/qa-report-${ARGUMENTS}.json. Verify both exist.'

component_chain.role: Normal-path artifact preflight — requires all 5 artifacts including qa-report with nested status==pass

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: The preflight validates these exact same-task files: ticket-<task-id>.md, context-<task-id>.json, dev-report-<task-id>.json, qa-report-<task-id>.json, and completion-<task-id>.md
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Guards /close from passing without a complete /dev cycle artifact chain
Guards /close --force from being self-invoked by a model

setup.reason: non-UI -- doc-only + config; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps, API endpoints). Changes are to slash-command documentation files only.

evidence.observed: 出现了无法close的现象 — there is a phenomenon where they can't be closed after /do development

measured.value: commands/close.md line 147: timestamp form requires 'docs/dev/qa-report-${ARGUMENTS}.json. Verify both exist.' AND commands/close.md line 64-65: forced path reuses 'the same Task-id resolution rules below'

expected.value: /close --force with any valid task-id form should succeed without requiring qa-report, since --force explicitly overrides artifact checks
expected.source: commands/close.md lines 96-97: 'This file MUST be written even if the task-id has no upstream BA spec / context / dev-report (per AC-CLOSE-FORCE-6)' — the forced path's own documentation acknowledges artifact absence as valid

evidence.gap: The forced-path acknowledgment that 'absence of upstream artifacts is intentional' (line 97) contradicts the inherited qa-report verification at line 147. The forced path breaks its own contract.

all_occurrences: commands/close.md:147 — qa-report verification in timestamp resolution
commands/close.md:64-65 — forced path inherits same resolution
commands/close.md:188 — existing /do awareness in inspector diff section
commands/close.md:97 — forced path explicitly permits absent upstream artifacts

reference_source.description: commands/close.md and commands/do.md read verbatim in this session

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts on this issue

context.codebase_state: commands/do.md: hook-only, 7 lines. commands/close.md: 386 lines, full close workflow. Neither modified by current session.
context.recent_commits: ca97137 auto-bulk: end-of-cycle commit for master

development_approach.strategy: Two-file fix: (1) In commands/close.md, add a one-sentence exception to the timestamp-form resolution section — when --force is present, skip qa-report verification and accept the timestamp as TASK_ID directly. (2) In commands/do.md, add a '## Closing /do-developed work' section documenting the /close path-form escape hatch.
files_to_modify: commands/close.md — task-id resolution section, timestamp branch: add forced-path exception
development_approach.validation_approach: Read both files after edit; verify the exception wording is present in close.md and the closing procedure section is present in do.md.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Round 1 (original spec): Codex identified 5 actionable issues and 1 observation — edit target, example command, forced-path qa-report inheritance, root cause wording, 'undocumented' precision. All 5 applied. Round 2 (QA-rejection response): Codex identified 6 findings on proposed two-location fix for 'do NOT branch' contradiction — (1) replace rather than append to avoid 'identically' vs 'exception' conflict; (2) reference mode not raw arg after --force is stripped; (3) preserve legacy ba-spec lookup wording; (4) scope the ac1 relaxation explicitly; (5) state normal-path unchanged; (6) AC2 not addressed (observation). Findings 1-5 applied, finding 6 is observation_only.

findings.issue: Proposed edit location is weak — commands/do.md body is not injected due to disable-model-invocation; close.md is the primary fix target
findings.proposed_fix: Put actionable guidance in commands/close.md under forced-override path, cross-reference from do.md
findings.rationale: Spec now specifies both files as Must Have: close.md as the logic fix, do.md as the cross-reference documentation

findings.issue: Example command omits explicit task-id/path — contradicts requirement that user must supply explicit task-id
findings.rationale: AC2 and Technical Hints now specify path form as primary, timestamp form as secondary (safe after AC1 fix)

findings.issue: Raw <task-id> timestamp form may still fail because forced path inherits qa-report verification from normal task-id resolution
findings.proposed_fix: Document path-form only, OR fix close.md forced resolution to skip qa-report check
findings.rationale: R1 now specifies the close.md logic fix (skip qa-report verification in forced-path timestamp branch). AC1 encodes this. The path-form is also documented as always-safe fallback.

findings.issue: Root cause wording too absolute — '/do produces NONE of these artifacts' is true but misses that a ticket may already exist
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to focus on the closure chain: context, dev-report, QA report, completion — not the ticket
findings.rationale: Root cause analysis now says '/dev closure chain: context, dev-report, QA report, and completion artifacts with matching task-id/status fields' — not a blanket 'none'

findings.issue: 'Escape hatch exists but is undocumented' is imprecise — /close --force is documented in close.md but not tied to /do workflow
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'documented generically in /close, but not surfaced for /do direct-development workflow'
findings.rationale: Context and Evidence sections now use precise wording about what is and isn't documented

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY (round 1): The draft correctly avoids synthetic /dev artifacts and fake QA/dev reports
findings.rationale: Won't Have section confirms this restraint is intentional

findings.issue: Round 2, Finding 1: 'rules reused identically' still conflicts with an 'Exception' parenthetical — sentence says reused identically then immediately creates exception
findings.proposed_fix: Replace the sentence rather than appending. Suggested: 'Reuse the same task-id form-detection and TASK_ID derivation rules below; do NOT create a separate path/timestamp/no-argument resolution algorithm for --force. Artifact existence checks are path-specific: when this resolution is invoked from the Forced-override path, skip qa-report-<task-id>.json verification.'
findings.rationale: AC1 THEN clause now specifies 'replace' rather than 'append'; Technical Hints specify 'replace' with suggested wording. Eliminates internally contradictory document state.

findings.issue: Round 2, Finding 2: 'when --force is also present' is ambiguous after --force is stripped in step 1
findings.proposed_fix: Refer to mode not raw arguments: 'When this resolution is invoked from the Forced-override path...'
findings.rationale: AC1 and Technical Hints now use 'invoked from the Forced-override path' wording, not 'when --force is present' after strip.

findings.issue: Round 2, Finding 3: 'ticket .md check still applies' underspecified — could accidentally drop legacy ba-spec support
findings.proposed_fix: Say: 'The ticket-<task-id>.md / legacy ba-spec-<task-id>.md resolution remains unchanged; only qa-report-<task-id>.json verification is skipped in forced mode.'
findings.rationale: AC1 THEN clause and Technical Hints now explicitly mention 'ticket-<task-id>.md / legacy ba-spec-<task-id>.md resolution remains unchanged'.

findings.issue: Round 2, Finding 4: Forced-path artifact-skip scope ambiguous — ticket check status unclear given close.md line 94 says forced path must write report even if upstream artifacts absent
findings.proposed_fix: Add clarifying sentence: 'This AC only relaxes the qa-report requirement for timestamp form; it does not change the existing ticket/legacy-ba-spec lookup requirement.'
findings.rationale: AC1 NOTE clause added stating this AC only relaxes qa-report; ticket lookup unchanged.

findings.issue: Round 2, Finding 5: Normal-path preservation not stated — dev could over-apply exception to normal /close <ts>
findings.proposed_fix: Add: 'Non-force timestamp resolution is unchanged: /close <task-id> without --force must still require qa-report-<task-id>.json and must still run the normal artifact preflight.'
findings.rationale: AC1 THEN clause and Technical Hints now contain explicit normal-path-unchanged statement.

findings.issue: Round 2, Finding 6 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): AC2 (commands/do.md documentation) not addressed by QA-rejection response — original spec already contains AC2
findings.rationale: AC2 was already in the original spec and was not the subject of QA objection. No change needed.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Round 1: all 5 actionable findings applied (edit target, example command, forced-path fix, root cause wording, precision). Round 2: all 5 actionable findings applied — (1) replace rather than append to avoid 'identically' contradiction; (2) mode-reference wording not raw arg; (3) legacy ba-spec preservation explicit; (4) scope relaxation stated; (5) normal-path unchanged stated.
